steps.title: Identify the claim to support
steps.content: The student claims that the many responses to Braschi's work, including music, comics, architecture, and furniture design, reflect Braschi's own way of making literature. The supporting quotation therefore needs to connect her cross-genre literary method with responses from other art forms.

steps.title: Reject irrelevant evidence
steps.content: Choice A says scholars studied linguistic and structural patterns in Braschi's work, but it does not connect her method to diverse creations by others. Choice C gives background about where she studied. Choice D describes her academic writing about other authors rather than responses inspired by her creative work.

steps.title: Choose the direct support
steps.content: The remaining quotation says Braschi pushes boundaries and blends genres within literature, inviting consideration of how other art forms might engage with literature. That directly supports the student's claim that the diverse creations reflect her own boundary-crossing approach. The answer is B.
